novel_features.name: Campaign health scorecard
novel_features.description: One-shot scorecard for every campaign — bounce rate, reply rate, silent-lead count, sender count, and a stale flag — without clicking through the dashboard.
novel_features.rationale: Joins campaigns, statistics, leads, and email accounts in the local store; the API has no campaign-health endpoint and needs 4+ calls per campaign.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first when an agent needs to know which campaigns are healthy before drilling into any one of them.

novel_features.name: Silent-lead finder
novel_features.description: Finds leads that were emailed but have not replied within N days — the exact set to follow up with or retire.
novel_features.rationale: Time-window query over local send/open/reply event timestamps; no SmartLead endpoint answers 'sent but no reply in N days'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to build a follow-up list instead of paging the whole lead set and diffing timestamps by hand.

novel_features.name: Cross-campaign dupe and domain ledger
novel_features.description: Scans the whole lead mirror for emails or domains that appear in two or more campaigns; --domain prints the full pitch ledger for one site.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-campaign join over the local leads table; the SmartLead API is campaign-scoped and cannot answer this in one call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this before adding leads to a campaign to avoid double-contacting a prospect already in flight.

novel_features.name: Sender health ranking
novel_features.description: Ranks every email sender account by a composite of inbox-warmup landing rate, SMTP/IMAP connection health, and sending utilization.
novel_features.rationale: Fleet-wide deliverability ranking computed from each account's warmup-stats and connection state — no SmartLead endpoint ranks the whole sender pool.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find which sender accounts are dragging deliverability before they tank a campaign.

novel_features.name: Warmup-readiness gate
novel_features.description: Checks each sender account against warmup thresholds (--min-days, --min-inbox-rate); with --strict it exits non-zero when any account fails — a scriptable launch gate.
novel_features.rationale: Turns per-account warmup-stats into a binary launch decision with a --strict exit code, usable as a CI-style gate.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Call this in a launch script to block attaching a sender account that is not warmed up yet.

novel_features.name: Reply-rate drift
novel_features.description: Computes week-over-week reply, open, and bounce deltas for a campaign by querying analytics one seven-day window at a time.
novel_features.rationale: The SmartLead API has no trend endpoint; the week-over-week deltas are computed here from per-window analytics queries.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to catch a campaign decaying over time rather than judging it from a single point-in-time stat.

narrative.headline: Every SmartLead API feature, plus a local mirror that answers campaign-health, silent-lead, and cross-campaign dedupe questions the API cannot.
narrative.value_prop: Beyond wrapping all 41 SmartLead REST endpoints with JSON-first, agent-native output, this CLI adds a local SQLite mirror no other SmartLead tool has. Sync once, then run health, silent, dupes, sender-health, warmup-gate, and drift offline — cross-campaign questions that otherwise cost a multi-call API loop per answer.
narrative.auth_narrative: SmartLead authenticates with an API key passed as the api_key query parameter. Set SMARTLEAD_API_KEY in your environment (find the key under Settings -> API in the SmartLead app). No OAuth, no login flow.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the API key is set and SmartLead is reachable before anything else.
Mirror campaigns, leads, email accounts, and statistics into the local SQLite store.
List every campaign now served instantly from the local mirror.
Get the bounce/reply/silent-lead scorecard across all campaigns at once.
Check whether a domain is already pitched before adding new leads.

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 429 rate limit errors during sync
troubleshoots.fix: The client auto-throttles and retries with backoff; if it persists, re-run sync later or lower --concurrency.

troubleshoots.symptom: sync returns no campaigns or empty tables
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm SMARTLEAD_API_KEY is exported and valid: run smartlead-pp-cli doctor.

troubleshoots.symptom: health or drift show stale numbers
troubleshoots.fix: Offline commands read the local mirror; re-run smartlead-pp-cli sync to refresh it.

troubleshoots.symptom: drift reports no history for a campaign
troubleshoots.fix: drift needs at least two synced snapshots over time; run sync on a schedule so history accumulates.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or operator needs to audit cold-email outreach in SmartLead: checking which campaigns are healthy, building follow-up lists of silent leads, deduplicating leads across campaigns before launch, or ranking sender accounts by deliverability. It is the right tool when the question spans multiple campaigns or needs week-over-week history, because those answers come from the local mirror rather than a single API call.

recipes.title: Morning campaign triage
recipes.explanation: Refresh the mirror, then get the all-campaigns health scorecard in one structured payload.

recipes.title: Pre-launch dedupe check
recipes.explanation: See every campaign, category, and last-contact date for a domain before pitching it again.

recipes.title: Build a follow-up list
recipes.explanation: Pull leads emailed but silent for 10+ days to feed into a follow-up sequence.

recipes.title: Trim a verbose campaign payload
recipes.explanation: SmartLead campaign objects are large; --agent with --select narrows the response to just the fields an agent needs.

recipes.title: Gate a launch on sender warmup
recipes.explanation: Returns a typed exit code so a launch script can block on a sender that is not warmed up.
